subject: LU-9341 llite: don't instantiate full layout on append

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

This is a tricky one, and could probably use a test.  I'm not sure what that should look like, though.

I'm fairly sure this is correct, I think I've convinced myself there's no need to hold the layout lock during the I/O, which was my original plan.

(1 comment)

1 style warning(s).

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

Actually the change doesn't match the commit message. You're not changing the instantiation on append only, but also changing the semantics of append write, which is pretty scary.

Patch Set 6:

(2 comments)

Jinshan,

Glad you were able to review this, thank you.

I asked a specific question in vvp_io.c, but assuming you're right and this breaks the append semantic... 

Would it be sufficient to take and hold the layout lock throughout, thereby avoiding the requirement to restart?  That seems like it should be enough.

Patch Set 6:

I can't really comment on the CLIO details, but the "write_append_truncate" test is good for finding bugs in this code. I would recommend to run it with the IO size in the same magnitude as the stripe size on a multi-striped file, so that you are exercising the code where clients are writing to different stripes, crossing stripe boundaries, etc.

Patch Set 6:

Will do, thank you Andreas.

I also sort of suspect this patch may be causing a racer failure...?  I will explore that as well.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

Well the current patch panics immediately with that test...  So, thank you, Andreas.  Definitely at least one bug to sort out here.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 6:

Almost certainly append write is used to write log. It's not worth the effort in my view ;-)

Patch Set 6:

(2 comments)

Hm.  I think we'll probably have to hold the layout lock throughout, which isn't the worst thing in the world.

I'm also considering ignoring it, like Jinshan suggested.  I'm going to ask around Cray and see whether or not people think it's important.

Patch Set 6:

Considering various things here...

Is there a particular reason why ll_layout_refresh does not check for LCK_EX metadata locks?  This has the effect that if the client updated the layout, it cannot use the EX lock for that and instead must go through the blocking callback process with the server.

Abandoned

Abandon in favor of https://review.whamcloud.com/35617

parents.subject: LU-10551 lod: obd_fid_alloc() could start a nested trans

commit.subject: LU-9341 llite: don't instantiate full layout on append
commit.message:
LU-9341 llite: don't instantiate full layout on append

Currently, append writes instantiate all layout components
to get around the fact that VFS does not provide the offset
for an append write and we can't check file size correctly
before knowing which components we must lock.

Instead of initializing all components, use our current
local size as the offset, then get the layout and
instantiate any required components as normal.  Now, we
lock these components and get the file size as normal in
vvp_io_write_start.

This file size may be more up to date than the local one,
in which case we must restart the I/O so we can instantiate
any components that we would touch at our new offset.

When we restart, we have a file size that is guaranteed to
have been correct at some point during our I/O, because the
only way for it to be wrong is a new component was
instantiated after we checked the layout, in which case
that instantiation happened during our I/O and is a
legitimate race condition.

Racing append writes can conceivably cause many restarts,
but the LDLM locking required should make this rare.

Note this does *NOT* change the LDLM layer locking behavior
of appends, which still take locks to infininity on all
components they are aware of, in order to protect file
size.

Add some tests for this to sanity-pfl.sh, correct other
tests which assume we can't append to incomplete files.

Cray-bug-id: LUS-5738
Change-Id: I757b8a0f32ca45c24c600f62d7cf5c50ad70fee6
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
